name: Computer Science
description: NUS Computer Science provides a strong foundation in software engineering, algorithms, artificial intelligence, computer systems, and data science. The programme is consistently ranked among the top in Asia and produces graduates highly sought after by the tech industry.

name: Business Administration
description: NUS Business Administration offers a broad-based business education covering finance, marketing, management, strategy, and analytics. Students can specialise in areas such as finance, marketing, or management and organisations.

name: Medicine
faculty: Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine
description: NUS Medicine is a five-year undergraduate programme that trains students to become competent and compassionate doctors. The curriculum integrates basic medical sciences with clinical training at the National University Hospital and other affiliated hospitals.

name: Law
description: NUS Law offers a four-year LLB programme that provides rigorous training in legal analysis, reasoning, and practice. The faculty is one of the most prestigious law schools in Asia with strong mooting and clinical legal education programmes.

name: Civil Engineering
description: NUS Civil Engineering prepares students for careers in structural, geotechnical, transportation, and environmental engineering. The programme emphasises sustainable infrastructure development and smart city technologies.

name: Electrical Engineering
description: NUS Electrical Engineering covers power systems, electronics, telecommunications, and control systems. Students gain skills in circuit design, signal processing, and embedded systems relevant to modern technology industries.

name: Mechanical Engineering
description: NUS Mechanical Engineering covers thermodynamics, mechanics, materials science, and manufacturing. Students learn to design and analyse mechanical systems for industries including aerospace, automotive, and energy.

name: Chemical Engineering
description: NUS Chemical Engineering combines chemistry, physics, mathematics, and engineering principles to solve problems related to the production and use of chemicals, fuels, drugs, food, and other products.

name: Economics
faculty: Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences
description: NUS Economics provides rigorous training in economic theory, quantitative methods, and policy analysis. Students develop strong analytical skills applicable to finance, consulting, government, and research.

name: Political Science
faculty: Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences
description: NUS Political Science examines political institutions, governance, international relations, and public policy. Students develop critical thinking and analytical skills for careers in government, diplomacy, and advocacy.

name: English Language and Literature
faculty: Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences
description: NUS English explores literature, linguistics, and cultural studies. Students develop advanced skills in critical analysis, communication, and creative expression through the study of diverse literary traditions.

name: Psychology
faculty: Faculty of Arts and Social Sciences
description: NUS Psychology covers cognitive, developmental, social, and clinical psychology. Students learn research methods and statistical analysis alongside psychological theory, preparing them for careers in counselling, HR, and research.

name: Data Science and Analytics
description: NUS Data Science and Analytics combines statistics, computer science, and domain expertise. Students learn data wrangling, machine learning, statistical modelling, and data visualisation for extracting insights from complex datasets.

name: Information Systems
description: NUS Information Systems bridges business and technology, training students to design and manage IT solutions for organisations. The curriculum covers systems analysis, project management, enterprise architecture, and digital innovation.

name: Accountancy
description: NUS Accountancy provides a professional accounting education accredited by ISCA, CPA Australia, and ACCA. Students learn financial reporting, auditing, taxation, and management accounting.

name: Life Sciences
description: NUS Life Sciences covers molecular biology, genetics, ecology, and biomedical science. Students gain laboratory skills and scientific reasoning applicable to research, healthcare, and the biotech industry.

name: Pharmacy
description: NUS Pharmacy is a four-year programme that trains students in pharmaceutical sciences, pharmacology, and clinical pharmacy practice. Graduates can register as pharmacists in Singapore.

name: Architecture
description: NUS Architecture is a five-year programme combining design studio work with architectural theory, technology, and history. Students develop skills in spatial design, sustainability, and urban planning.

name: Industrial Design
description: NUS Industrial Design trains students in product design, user experience, and design thinking. The programme combines creative studio practice with engineering and technology for human-centred design solutions.

name: Computer Science
faculty: School of Computer Science and Engineering
description: NTU Computer Science offers a comprehensive curriculum in algorithms, software engineering, AI, cybersecurity, and computer networks. The programme is well-regarded for its strong industry connections and research output.

name: Computer Engineering
faculty: School of Computer Science and Engineering / School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: NTU Computer Engineering integrates hardware and software expertise, covering embedded systems, computer architecture, IoT, and real-time computing. Graduates are well-positioned for roles bridging hardware and software.

name: Business
description: NTU Business offers a comprehensive business education with specialisations in banking and finance, marketing, human resource consulting, and entrepreneurship. The programme is triple-accredited (AACSB, EQUIS, AMBA).

name: Accountancy
description: NTU Accountancy provides professional training in financial accounting, auditing, taxation, and corporate governance. The programme is accredited by ISCA, CPA Australia, and ACCA for direct professional qualification pathways.

name: Biological Sciences
description: NTU Biological Sciences covers molecular biology, genetics, cell biology, and ecology. The programme offers specialisations in biomedical sciences and environmental biology with strong laboratory training.

name: Chemistry and Biological Chemistry
faculty: School of Physical and Mathematical Sciences
description: NTU Chemistry and Biological Chemistry provides rigorous training in organic, inorganic, physical, and analytical chemistry. Students gain strong laboratory skills for careers in chemical and pharmaceutical industries.

name: Mechanical Engineering
faculty: School of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
description: NTU Mechanical Engineering covers mechanics, thermodynamics, manufacturing, and robotics. The programme emphasises hands-on design projects and offers specialisations in areas such as robotics and energy systems.

name: Electrical and Electronic Engineering
faculty: School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering
description: NTU Electrical and Electronic Engineering covers power systems, microelectronics, signal processing, and communications. Students can specialise in areas including power engineering, intelligent systems, and nanoelectronics.

name: Civil Engineering
faculty: School of Civil and Environmental Engineering
description: NTU Civil Engineering covers structural engineering, geotechnics, water resources, and transportation. The programme prepares students for careers in infrastructure development and environmental engineering.

name: Aerospace Engineering
faculty: School of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering
description: NTU Aerospace Engineering covers aerodynamics, propulsion, flight mechanics, and avionics. Students gain expertise in aircraft and spacecraft design, maintenance, and systems integration relevant to Singapore's aviation industry.

name: Materials Engineering
faculty: School of Materials Science and Engineering
description: NTU Materials Engineering covers metals, polymers, ceramics, and nanomaterials. Students learn to develop and characterise advanced materials for applications in electronics, biomedicine, and energy.

name: Communication Studies
faculty: Wee Kim Wee School of Communication and Information
description: NTU Communication Studies covers journalism, advertising, public relations, broadcasting, and cinema. Students develop strong media production and strategic communication skills for the creative industries.

name: Linguistics and Multilingual Studies
description: NTU Linguistics and Multilingual Studies explores language structure, cognitive linguistics, translation, and multilingualism. Students develop expertise in language analysis and multilingual communication relevant to Singapore's diverse context.

name: Art, Design and Media
faculty: School of Art, Design and Media
description: NTU Art, Design and Media offers specialisations in photography and digital imaging, animation, product design, interactive media, and visual communication. The programme balances creative practice with design theory.

name: Economics
description: NTU Economics trains students in microeconomics, macroeconomics, econometrics, and applied economics. The programme emphasises quantitative methods and data analysis for economic policy and business applications.

name: Psychology
description: NTU Psychology covers cognitive, developmental, social, and clinical psychology. The programme emphasises research methods and evidence-based practice for understanding human behaviour and mental processes.

name: Business Management
faculty: Lee Kong Chian School of Business
description: SMU Business Management offers a broad-based business education with majors in finance, marketing, operations management, organisational behaviour, and strategic management. Known for its seminar-style interactive pedagogy.

name: Accountancy
description: SMU Accountancy is one of the top accounting programmes in Asia, offering professional training in financial accounting, audit, tax, and data analytics. The programme provides direct pathways to CPA and ACCA qualifications.

name: Economics
description: SMU Economics focuses on applied economics with strong quantitative training. Students learn econometrics, behavioural economics, and policy analysis through SMU's distinctive seminar-style teaching approach.

name: Information Systems
faculty: School of Computing and Information Systems
description: SMU Information Systems trains students in software development, cybersecurity, data management, and digital business transformation. The programme is known for producing graduates with strong technical and business acumen.

name: Law
faculty: Yong Pung How School of Law
description: SMU Law offers a four-year LLB programme with a distinctive focus on commercial and corporate law, dispute resolution, and cross-border transactions. The seminar-style teaching develops strong advocacy and communication skills.

name: Social Sciences
description: SMU Social Sciences offers majors in politics and public policy, psychology, and sociology. The programme develops critical thinking and research skills through SMU's interactive seminar-based pedagogy.

name: Computing and Law
faculty: School of Computing and Information Systems / Yong Pung How School of Law
description: SMU Computing and Law is a unique double-degree programme combining computer science with legal studies. Graduates are uniquely positioned for roles at the intersection of technology and law, including tech regulation, IP law, and legal tech.
